definition: The consensus 'sticky above-target' inflation view is the most crowded macro view per BofA Global Fund Manager Survey, with CFTC net short Treasuries near 2-year extremes and 5Y breakevens near 2.4-2.5%. A contrarian disinflation scenario (core PCE 2.3-2.5% for 2-3 months) offers a 3:1 to 5:1 payoff ratio because the consensus outcome is already priced.
evidence: BofA Global Fund Manager Survey: 'above-target inflation' is most crowded macro view
CFTC net short Treasuries near 2-year extremes
Contrarian scenario: 50-100bp Treasury rally vs. 10-20bp remaining return to consensus — 3:1 to 5:1 payoff ratio
pair_3 calls this 'the single most actionable insight'
open_questions: What is the probability of a contrarian disinflation scenario materializing?
How quickly would positioning unwind on 2-3 months of below-consensus prints?
What is the optimal structure for expressing this asymmetry?
